Assistant2026-07-03T22:36:09.796Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-03T22:36:09.842Z

Audit results

Tests are green (246/246) and the core discipline is genuinely good — strict tool schemas, adaptive thinking with output_config.effort, no forbidden sampling params, typed exception mapping, single-writer applyPatch. But I found five real API/context bugs (two of them undermine the product's own design goals) and seven visual/UX issues, two of which are visible within the first three turns of a demo session.

API / spec issues

1. The model never sees the user's prior answers — and the system prompt claims it does. systemPrompt.ts:23 tells the model the event log contains "recent user messages and your own past actions." It doesn't: turnRunner.ts:246 only ever appends update_beliefs/interact/export_design_md events (the message and session_start kinds in beliefState.ts:81 are dead), and contextAssembly.ts:90 replays prior turns as tool_use/tool_result pairs only — the user's actual answers are dropped from the history. So on turn N the model sees its own questions but none of the user's earlier replies except the latest. The "contradiction → clarify" behavior the interview strategy depends on only works when the contradiction is in the current message.

2. Conversation-level prompt caching is broken every turn. Turn N's request folds the tool-result acks and the turn payload into one user message (contextAssembly.ts:126); turn N+1 replays that message without the payload text. The prefix bytes diverge right there, so only system+tools ever cache — the observed constant cache_read ≈ 5754 in GATE_RESULTS.md is exactly that, not the "conversation-level cache prefix preserved turn over turn" the file's comment claims. The whole conversation history is reprocessed at full price every turn. Fixing this and #1 is one restructure: put the user's answer in its own stable text block (replayed verbatim in history) and the volatile belief-state/event-tail/instruction block last, behind the cache breakpoint — the prefix becomes append-only, and the model regains the user's words.

3. Event-id collisions after server restart. eventLog.ts:20 uses a module-global counter with a fixed "e" seed. The client persists belief state in localStorage across server restarts (and dev-mode reloads); the counter resets to zero, so a resumed session gets a second e01, e02… appended to a log that already contains them. Provenance and rationale evidence arrays reference events by id, so duplicates silently corrupt the audit trail. The counter is also shared across concurrent sessions. Fix: derive the next id from the incoming state's existing events (max + 1) or use per-request entropy.

4. Streaming is a no-op — the latency-masking design goal is unmet. The wire format promises delta = "NL text of interact, streamed as it arrives," but turnRunner.ts:206 subscribes to stream.on("text"), which only fires for plain text blocks — and the system prompt instructs "your only outputs are structured tool calls." Result: zero delta events in all 15 recorded live turns; users stare at a static state for the full multi-second turn. Either stream the interact tool's input_json_delta (partial-JSON parse of the question field) or drop the pretense and ship a proper thinking indicator. Related nit: on a corrective retry, any deltas from the rejected attempt have already been flushed to the client.

5. Export turns fabricate history. The server deliberately returns no priorTurnRecord for an export turn (turnRunner.ts:310), but realTurnAgent.ts:104 unconditionally pushes one built from the synthesized ask interaction. If the user keeps chatting after an export, the replayed history claims the model called interact with a question it never asked, instead of export_design_md.

Minor/hygiene: turn-2+ requests start with an assistant message (docs say first message must be user — it demonstrably worked live for 15 turns, but the fix for #2 makes the history start with the kickoff user message anyway, removing the exposure); session_start is never emitted despite V0_PLAN promising it; RealTurnAgent labels turn 1's record turn-2-* (cosmetic).

Visual / UX issues

1. Proposal variants overflow the chat column (desktop: measured scrollWidth 203 vs clientWidth 170 per tile, horizontal scrollbar on the transcript; at tablet width the cards overlap neighboring bubbles completely). card.default/nav.default have intrinsic min-widths (240/320px) that don't fit a 2-across grid in the 420px column — ProposalPicker.tsx:131 needs min-w-0/overflow-hidden plus a single-column (or scaled) layout for wide components.

2. No auto-scroll in the chat. MessageList.tsx:49 has no scroll anchoring at all — once the transcript exceeds the viewport, new questions and proposals appear below the fold with no cue.

3. Machine text leaks into the user's own chat bubble. Picking a variant renders user picked "Muted green" for the color.primary proposal on button.primary as if the user typed it. The NormalizedMessage text is for the model; the transcript should show a friendly rendering ("Picked: Muted green"). Same concern applies to [control]/[region …] prefixed messages.

4. The export modal looks unfinished: a light unthemed panel with a detached dark "Close" strip floating over both panes (Session.tsx:365 wraps the intentionally shell-agnostic ExportPanel without giving it a unified surface). Needs one composed modal surface in shell chrome.

5. No responsive floor. Fixed rail + fixed 420px chat means below ~1200px the preview collapses to a sliver and at 768px the layout shatters. Even for a desktop-only demo, a min-width on the app root with graceful horizontal scroll would prevent the broken states.

6. The DEBUG inspector is always visible in the chat column footer — gate DevInspectorPanel behind a query param or env flag.

7. Sessions rail records a duplicate entry on every mount (a fresh crypto.randomUUID() per mount, upserted once a transcript exists — my one demo run produced two "a booking app for dog groo…" entries), and clicking a past session is a silent no-op. Either wire per-session restore or make the rail read-only with the dead affordance removed.

Plan

Phase 1 — context/caching correctness (server, one coherent change): Restructure assembleContext so each turn's user message is [tool_results, text(user answer — stable, replayed), text(belief state + event tail + instruction — volatile, cache breakpoint)], replay the stable blocks verbatim, and start history from the kickoff user message. Log message (and session_start) events in turnRunner. Update the system prompt's event-log description to match reality. Fix event-id generation to derive from existing state. Fix the export-turn priorTurnRecord on the client. Verify with unit tests on byte-stable prefixes; then re-record one live interview to confirm cache_read_input_tokens grows turn-over-turn (needs your API key/budget sign-off).

Phase 2 — first-screen UX bugs (client): Auto-scroll anchoring in MessageList; friendly transcript rendering for picks/control/region messages; proposal-variant sizing (min-w-0, single-column for card/nav targets); a visible "thinking" indicator (and optionally real interact-input streaming server-side).

Phase 3 — polish: Compose the export modal as one themed surface; gate the debug inspector; de-dupe the sessions rail and remove/implement the dead click; add a layout min-width; tidy scrollbar styling on the dark theme.

Each phase is independently committable with npm test green, matching the checkpoint discipline in AGENTS.md. Nothing here touches the frozen src/contracts/ except optionally adding a streamed-question delta (which the existing delta event already accommodates). Want me to start on Phase 1?
